name: Pico La Era Loop
name_original: Parque Natural Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche – trail run circular al Pico La Era
description: This difficult 7.3-mile trail run at Pico La Era offers challenging climbs and spectacular views across the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aro
region_intro: Jogging routes around Linares De La Sierra are situated within the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park, offering a diverse landscape for runners. The region is characterized by verdant, humid environments with wooded valleys featuring evergreen oak, holm oak, and chestnut trees. Numerous paths and trails wind through this varied terrain, often following rivers and ancient irrigation channels. The area's elevation, around 500 meters above sea level, provides scenic views of the surrounding hills. Best jogging routes around Linares De La Sierra The most popular jogging route is Pico La Era Loop, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation changes and offers views across the natural park. Another top favourite among local runners is Alájar and Santa Ana la Real Loop, a difficult 9.3 miles (14.9 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain within the natural park, providing a challenging running experience. Local runners also love the Running loop from Linares de la Sierra, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail leading through wooded areas and past natural features, often completed in about 1 hour 26 minutes. Jogging around Linares De La Sierra is defined by wooded valleys, varied terrain, and the presence of numerous streams and rivers. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Linares De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Linares De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Linares de la Sierra?
A: There are over 50 dedicated running routes around Linares de la Sierra, offering a wide range of options for joggers. These routes are situated within the beautiful Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Linares de la Sierra?
A: Jogging routes in Linares de la Sierra feature diverse terrain, primarily winding through verdant, humid environments with wooded valleys. You'll encounter evergreen oak, holm oak, acorn, and chestnut trees. Many paths follow streams, rivers, and ancient Moorish irrigation channels (acequias), providing a lush and scenic experience. The area's elevation, around 500 meters above sea level, also offers scenic views of the surrounding hills.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Linares de la Sierra?
A: The running routes in Linares de la Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. Over 200 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the natural beauty and tranquil atmosphere of the trails within the natural park.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Linares de la Sierra?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984466988/pico-la-era-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico La Era Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984506054/alajar-and-santa-ana-la-real-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alájar and Santa Ana la Real Loop&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options, offering challenging runs with significant elevation changes and scenic views.

Q: Are there any jogging routes that pass by waterfalls or interesting water features?
A: Yes, the region is known for its numerous streams and the Arroyo Plamencia, which contribute to the verdant landscape. The ancient acequias (Moorish irrigation channels) also create unique paths. You can find a beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7287170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which some routes may pass near or lead to, offering a refreshing element to your run.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for running routes in Linares de la Sierra?
A: The jogging routes in Linares de la Sierra cater to various fitness levels, though the majority are classified as moderate to difficult. There are 20 moderate routes and 30 difficult routes available. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984466988/pico-la-era-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico La Era Loop&lt;/a&gt; are considered difficult due to significant elevation changes, while others like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31450109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Linares de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; offer a moderate challenge.

Q: Can I find family-friendly jogging routes in Linares de la Sierra?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' for jogging, some of the moderate trails might be suitable for families with older children who are accustomed to running or hiking. The 'Rutas del Agua' mentioned in regional research, for instance, is a moderate 9-kilometer circular route that follows rivers and ancient irrigation channels, providing a lush and scenic experience that could be enjoyable for active families.

Q: Are the running trails in Linares de la Sierra suitable for dogs?
A: Linares de la Sierra is located within the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park. Generally, dogs are permitted on trails within natural parks, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Check local park regulations for any specific rules regarding dogs.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I might encounter on a run?
A: Many routes offer scenic views of the surrounding hills and the natural park. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2894628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alájar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arias Montano Peak and the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Angels&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotto of the Marvels&lt;/a&gt; in Aracena, which could be visited before or after your run.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Linares de la Sierra?
A: The higher elevation of Linares de la Sierra (around 500-505 meters) provides cooler temperatures than coastal areas, making it pleasant for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with mild weather and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be warm, but early morning or late evening runs are still enjoyable. Winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31452411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Marines Village – Alájar Viewpoint loop from Los Marines&lt;/a&gt; offer a significant distance of over 23 km with substantial elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31451145" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Aracena – Aracena town centre loop from Aracena&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 19 km long and features considerable ascents.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the jogging trails in Linares de la Sierra?
A: Linares de la Sierra is a small village, and while public transport options might be limited, it is generally accessible by bus from larger towns like Aracena. It's recommended to check local bus schedules in advance, as services may be infrequent, especially on weekends or holidays. Many visitors prefer to arrive by car for greater flexibility in accessing various trailheads.

Q: Are there places to park near the running routes in Linares de la Sierra?
A: As a small village, Linares de la Sierra typically has parking available within or on the outskirts of the village, which serves as a common starting point for many trails. For specific trailheads further afield, parking availability can vary, so it's advisable to check route details on komoot or local signage for designated parking areas.
tips:
One of the most popular routes to reach Pico La Era begins in the village of Los Marines. This route is relatively easy and has a distance of about 4.3 kilometres, with a maximum gradient of 161 metres. Along the way, hikers can enjoy the typical vegetation of the area, including chestnut trees, holm oaks and strawberry trees.

From the top of the peak, you can admire impressive panoramic views, including the geodesic vertex that marks the highest point.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this trail run?
faqs.answer: This trail is categorized as a "difficult" run and requires very good fitness due to its demanding nature, including significant elevation changes over 11.8 kilometers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail run?
faqs.answer: While it's a trail run, the route is described as "Mostly paved." This typically means well-maintained, stable paths, possibly packed earth or gravel, rather than rugged, technical trails. You'll experience diverse natural park terrain, including areas with chestnut, holm oak, and cork trees.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect spectacular views of the heart of the Parque Natural Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche, especially from the summit of La Era Peak. From there, you'll clearly see the villages of Alájar, Linares de la Sierra, and Navahermosa, set within the distinctive 'dehesa' landscape.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the primary landmark is the summit of La Era Peak itself, where you'll find a geodesic vertex. This point offers panoramic views of the surrounding natural park and villages.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through sections of several official trails, including Tierra de los Descubrimientos, Alájar-Linares de la Sierra, [CIMA HL07] Alájar * Linares de la Sierra, and Camino de Linares a Aracena.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route around La Era Peak. While specific parking information for the trailhead isn't provided, visitors typically start from nearby villages like Alájar or Linares de la Sierra, where parking options are usually available.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche offers green landscapes throughout the year due to its 'dehesa' ecosystem. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for trail running, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Parque Natural Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche generally welcomes dogs, but they should always be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and respect other trail users. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Parque Natural Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche. However, it's always good practice to respect the natural environment and follow park regulations.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Parque Natural Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche, which is known for its charming whitewashed villages. Nearby towns like Alájar, Linares de la Sierra, and Aracena offer various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail run?
faqs.answer: For an average trail runner, this 11.8-kilometer circular route with over 400 meters of elevation gain typically takes around 1 hour and 35 minutes to complete.
